questions.label: Why is this my week strategy?
questions.response_template: Explain the current weekly strategy using readiness_status, trigger_reason, and coach_notes.

questions.label: Why did volume change?
questions.response_template: Explain volume adjustments using volume_mode, volume_adjustment_pct, trigger_reason, and readiness context.

questions.label: Why did volume stay the same?
questions.response_template: Explain why no major adjustment was needed and connect it to stable readiness or data.

questions.label: What is my progression focus this week?
questions.response_template: Explain progression_focus and how the user should approach the training week.

questions.label: Why am I in a deload week?
questions.response_template: Use deload_flag, readiness_status, and recovery context to explain why a deload was triggered.

questions.label: What if I feel better than this plan suggests?
questions.response_template: Explain why the plan still follows logged data and encourage consistency over impulsive changes.

questions.label: What if I feel worse than this plan suggests?
questions.response_template: Explain how the user should use judgment while still respecting the program structure and logging accuracy.

questions.label: How does readiness change the week?
questions.response_template: Explain how readiness_status affects intensity, volume, and coaching recommendations.

questions.label: What drives weekly adjustments?
questions.response_template: Explain which weekly signals influence changes, such as recovery, completion, and logged data.

questions.label: How should I approach this week overall?
questions.response_template: Give a practical summary of the week's training mindset using coach_notes, training_note, and readiness context.

questions.label: Why is this exercise here?
questions.response_template: Explain the likely role of the selected movement in the workout structure.

questions.label: What should I focus on for this movement?
questions.response_template: Give execution intent such as control, stretch, stability, tension, or output.

questions.label: What is the goal of this exercise?
questions.response_template: Explain whether the movement is for main stimulus, accessory work, skill, or fatigue-efficient volume.

questions.label: Why is this placed here in the workout?
questions.response_template: Explain why an exercise may appear earlier or later based on fatigue, stability, and priority.

questions.label: Should I push hard or control the reps here?
questions.response_template: Explain whether the emphasis should be on effort, control, or clean execution.

questions.label: What makes this different from similar movements?
questions.response_template: Explain how the movement may provide a slightly different training effect than another common exercise.

questions.label: How should this exercise feel when done right?
questions.response_template: Describe the intended muscle feel and execution quality in general terms.

questions.label: Is this a main lift or accessory movement?
questions.response_template: Classify the movement's role in the session structure.

questions.label: What common mistake should I avoid here?
questions.response_template: Give a simple deterministic coaching caution focused on control and intent.

questions.label: How does this help the rest of the workout?
questions.response_template: Explain how the movement supports the full session and broader training goal.

questions.label: How do I improve coaching accuracy?
questions.response_template: Explain that complete and consistent logging improves deterministic recommendations.

questions.label: What counts as a completed workout?
questions.response_template: Explain what should qualify as completed in a practical product sense.

questions.label: What does low data mean?
questions.response_template: Explain data_quality_flag and why missing or inconsistent data weakens guidance.

questions.label: Why does logging consistency matter?
questions.response_template: Explain why trend quality matters more than one-off entries.

questions.label: What should I log every time?
questions.response_template: Explain which fields matter most for stable recommendations.

questions.label: What happens if I skip logs?
questions.response_template: Explain how incomplete data reduces confidence and can flatten the coaching quality.

questions.label: Does one bad entry matter?
questions.response_template: Explain that one bad entry usually matters less than repeated inconsistency.

questions.label: How often should I log for best results?
questions.response_template: Give practical guidance on frequency and consistency.

questions.label: Why does the system need my inputs?
questions.response_template: Explain how deterministic logic relies on user-reported data.

questions.label: How do I get better recommendations?
questions.response_template: Explain that better recommendations come from better logs, workout completion, and consistent behavior.

questions.label: Why did calories change?
questions.response_template: Explain calorie_adjustment and connect it to weekly performance or trend context.

questions.label: Why did calories stay the same?
questions.response_template: Explain why current intake may still be aligned with the weekly trend.

questions.label: How does nutrition support this week?
questions.response_template: Explain how nutrition supports recovery, performance, and the current weekly goal.

questions.label: Should I change food quality or just calories?
questions.response_template: Explain the difference between total intake and food quality in a practical way.

questions.label: What if my hunger is high this week?
questions.response_template: Give practical guidance while keeping the user aligned with the current plan.

questions.label: What if my appetite is low this week?
questions.response_template: Explain how to stay consistent with the plan despite reduced appetite.

questions.label: Do calories affect recovery?
questions.response_template: Explain the relationship between intake and recovery quality.

questions.label: How should I think about calorie adjustments?
questions.response_template: Explain that calorie changes are strategic nudges, not random fluctuations.

questions.label: What if the scale is not moving how I expected?
questions.response_template: Explain how the system interprets trend data and why patience matters.

questions.label: What should I do nutrition-wise this week?
questions.response_template: Give a simple practical weekly nutrition summary based on adjustment and coaching context.

questions.label: How does recovery affect this workout?
questions.response_template: Explain how recovery state changes training recommendations and session feel.

questions.label: What should I do on a deload week?
questions.response_template: Explain how to train during a deload and what the week is meant to accomplish.

questions.label: What does low recovery change?
questions.response_template: Explain how low recovery influences volume, intensity, and expectations.

questions.label: Why does recovery matter so much?
questions.response_template: Explain why recovery is central to good progression.

questions.label: What if I am sore but still can train?
questions.response_template: Explain how to distinguish manageable fatigue from a real need to pull back.

questions.label: What if my sleep has been bad lately?
questions.response_template: Explain how poor sleep impacts readiness and why it matters in the system.

questions.label: Can I push through low recovery?
questions.response_template: Give a practical but cautious response about respecting the plan.

questions.label: How should I adjust when I feel run down?
questions.response_template: Explain how to stay within the session while managing effort intelligently.

questions.label: What is the goal of a lower-recovery week?
questions.response_template: Explain the purpose of reducing strain to preserve long-term progress.

questions.label: How can I support recovery better?
questions.response_template: Give practical recovery priorities like sleep, food, consistency, and not overshooting effort.

questions.label: How are workouts built?
questions.response_template: Explain the deterministic workout-building logic at a high level.

questions.label: Why isn't this random?
questions.response_template: Explain why structure beats randomness and why the app is intentionally rule-based.

questions.label: How does the system choose exercises?
questions.response_template: Explain that exercise selection follows structured rules, split logic, and progression intent.

questions.label: Why is workout structure important?
questions.response_template: Explain why ordering and role assignment matter in a good session.

questions.label: How does the program stay consistent?
questions.response_template: Explain how repeatable rules create dependable training.

questions.label: What makes this system deterministic?
questions.response_template: Explain that outputs are driven by fixed rules and logged inputs rather than AI generation.

questions.label: Why not just generate anything?
questions.response_template: Explain why controlled logic is better than novelty for training outcomes.

questions.label: How does the program progress over time?
questions.response_template: Explain how trends and weekly context shape progression.

questions.label: What should I expect from this system?
questions.response_template: Explain what the user should expect from the app over repeated use.

questions.label: Why does the app ask for my weekly data?
questions.response_template: Explain why the system needs weekly inputs to tailor deterministic guidance.
